programme.title: Xploration Outer Space S2 - "Space This Amusement Park"
programme.desc: Join Emily Calandrelli and astronaut Jon McBride as they discover how amusement park rides can be just like space travel.

programme.title: Sci Q S1 - "Test Your Science Genius"
programme.desc: Fast-paced, pop-science show that will test your science I.Q. Do flying squirrels really fly? What caused the extinction of the dinosaur? How old are the fossils found at the bottom of the Grand Canyon?

programme.title: Sci Q S1 - "What’s Your Science IQ?"
programme.desc: Fast-paced, pop-science show that will test your science I.Q. How smart is the modern-day robot? How come Earth has a protective atmosphere? Why does the U.S. have more tornadoes than anywhere else in the world?

programme.title: Sci Q S1 - "Discover your Science Intelligence"
programme.desc: Fast-paced, pop-science show that will test your science I.Q. Is it true that we only use 10% of our brain? Did Mars used to have an ocean? How much longer will we be able to live on fossil fuels?

programme.title: Sci Q S1 - "Measure Your Sci Q"
programme.desc: Fast-paced, pop-science show that will test your science I.Q. What causes a lightning strike? Where in our solar system is the asteroid belt? What’s the difference between a stalagmite and a stalactite?

programme.title: Xploration DIY Sci S5 - "Garage Science"
programme.desc: Host Steve Spangler and his sidekick Higginsworth explore science experiments you can do in your own garage.

programme.title: Xploration DIY Sci S5 - "Art Science"
programme.desc: Host Steve Spangler and his sidekick Higginsworth explore new ways to create art, using science.

programme.title: Xploration DIY Sci S5 - "Classroom: Impossible"
programme.desc: Fourth grade teacher Sandra Sanchez has a problem – her class is a year behind in science after a season of virtual-only teaching.

programme.title: Xploration DIY Sci S5 - "Museum Of Science, Boston #1"
programme.desc: Host Steve Spangler and his sidekick Higginsworth explore the Museum of Science, Boston, for new do-it-yourself science tricks and experiments.

programme.title: Combat Forces S1 - "Reconnaissance"
programme.desc: Reconnaissance units operate behind enemy lines, using elite training to gather vital intelligence and expose an enemy’s strengths and weaknesses.

programme.title: Combat Forces S1 - "Coalition Training"
programme.desc: Allied forces train together to build teamwork and coordination, ensuring success in combat zones like Iraq and Afghanistan.

programme.title: Combat Forces S1 - "Damage Control School"
programme.desc: NATO sailors train to fight fire and floods at sea, mastering damage control under extreme heat, cold, and pressure to save their ship.

programme.title: Combat Forces S1 - "Navy Boarding Party"
programme.desc: Modern naval boarding teams train to enforce embargoes, stop smugglers, and confront armed pirates in some of the toughest maritime operations.

programme.title: Health Explored S2 - "Open Air Life Part 1"
programme.desc: Travel to Tromso to learn about the concept of "Open Air Life" from the inhabitants of this arctic region.

programme.title: Health Explored S2 - "Open Air Life Part 2"
programme.desc: Go sea to summit with a skiing experience, as we trek to an unskiied peak, then go diving in the frozen ocean.

programme.title: Health Explored S2 - "Sands of Time Part 1"
programme.desc: From environmental health to physical health, discover what coastal life is like in Morocco before heading inland to the desert.

programme.title: Secret Wars Uncovered S1 - "The Gaza Offensive"
programme.desc: A mixture of political frustration and violent challenges led to the Gaza Offensive in 2014. As so many times over the past 13 years, Israeli soldiers launched brutally into the streets of Gaza, leaving destruction and death in their wake.

programme.title: War Machines S1 - "Mastering Arms"
programme.desc: A weapon that has built Empires, toppled Kingdoms, and slaughtered millions, the story of the gun and how it has evolved throughout the centuries is long.

programme.title: War Machines S1 - "Advancing Destruction"
programme.desc: This episode tells the story of that journey showcasing both big and the small artillery and the impact they have had on warfare.

programme.title: War Machines S1 - "Triumphant On Land"
programme.desc: Highlighting some of the most influential movers ever to take to the battlefield including some of the most infamous tanks.

programme.title: Combat Forces S1 - "Simulated Warfare"
programme.desc: Modern tech is reshaping battlefields and training. This episode explores simulators and tools that prepare soldiers for combat.

programme.title: Combat Forces S1 - "Full Spectrum Warfare"
programme.desc: Modern warfare spans land, sea, and air. This episode explores training for Full Spectrum Warfare across all environments.

programme.title: Combat Forces S1 - "Airborne Artillery"
programme.desc: Airborne artillery brings heavy firepower behind enemy lines. At Fort Bragg, we witness elite crews train with precision, strategy, and skill.

programme.title: Dictators' Hideouts S1 - "Yanukovych's Hidden Palace"
programme.desc: Having stolen billions from the populace, Ukrainian dictator Viktor Yanukovych hides his treasure trove in a mind-bending corruption scheme that leads to Mezhyhirya, a 150-acre estate, with secret underground tunnels.

programme.title: Dictators' Hideouts S1 - "Ceausescu's Delusions of Grandeur"
programme.desc: Casa Poporlui is the magnum opus of Romania’s most bloodthirsty dictator, Nicolae Ceausescu, and hides secrets of an extensive underground bunker system.

programme.title: Future Food S1 - "Episode 5"
programme.desc: Nigerian Minister of Agriculture Akinwunmi Ayo Adesina believes it is his job to ensure Nigerians eat food grown in Nigeria, and he's determined to overcome the obstacles to Nigerian self-sufficiency in food production.

programme.title: Future Food S1 - "Episode 6"
programme.desc: Shanghai-based social media agency Resonance apply their branding skills to convince young Chinese that farming could be a viable career choice.

programme.title: Journey to Planet Earth S2 - "Future Conditional"
programme.desc: Looks at the spread of air and water pollution and how contamination in one area can affect people and animals living thousands of miles away.

programme.title: Space Greed S1 - "Suing Our Way To Space"
programme.desc: Cowboy hat-wearing billionaires have reasons for their multi-billion dollar investment in space exploration but it’s hard to ignore how much money they stand to make.

programme.title: Space Greed S1 - "Will Satellites Help Us Save or Exploit Earth?"
programme.sub-title: Will Satellites Help Us Save or Exploit Earth?
programme.desc: Our long-term survival on this planet depends on advanced satellites keeping us connected and keeping an eye on the Earth from orbit.

programme.title: Space Greed S1 - "Space Pirates"
programme.desc: Between advanced satellites and tons of precious resources the future of space exploration looks to be highly profitable.

programme.title: Space Greed S1 - "Mad Scientists In Outer Space"
programme.desc: The unique properties of space make it ideal for some world-changing and unique scientific experiments.

programme.title: Power & S3 - "Boxing - Ruqsana Begum"
programme.desc: Ruqsana Begum is a Muay Tai world champion who wants to become the next world champion in boxing. She’s wants to inspire Muslim women and make a difference.

programme.title: Power & S3 - "MMA - Nick Hein"
programme.desc: Nick Hein is a professional Mixed Martial Artist from Cologne and signed with the UFC. His ultimate goal is to become MMA world champion.

programme.title: Power & S3 - "Extreme Martial Arts – Shaina West"
programme.desc: Shaina West taught herself martial arts by watching Youtube recovering from a motorcycle accident. The videos she posted went viral and ‘The Samurider’ was born.

programme.title: Power & S3 - "Karate – James Pankiewicz"
programme.desc: Englishman and karateka James Pankiewicz came to Okinawa, the birthplace of Karate, for his wife and to open a Dojo and the immensely popular Dojo Bar.

programme.title: Breakthrough S1 - "Coronavirus: Don't Blame Bats"
programme.desc: Bats are getting their share of the blame for the COVID-19 pandemic, but are they really at fault?

programme.title: Breakthrough S1 - "Peru's Mysterious Cloud Warriors"
programme.desc: Archeologists and geneticists are uncovering surprising details about one of the most mysterious civilizatiions in Pre-Incan South America.

programme.title: Breakthrough S1 - "Decoding Megalodon"
programme.desc: Scientists have concluded that "meg" was able to achieve its gargantuan proportions by starting its carnivorous diet early -- in the womb.

programme.title: Breakthrough S1 - "Noah's Ark for the Humans"
programme.desc: Scientists believe that within a few decades many useful microbes will have disappeared so they want to create a vault for Human Microbiota.

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Hallucinogens"
programme.desc: While psychedelics were originally seen as a tool for spiritual enlightenment and healing, new research and studies are revisiting psychedelics to reveal their potential clinical and therapeutic impact.

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Music"
programme.desc: We use music to nurture our children, comfort the sick, and worship the unseen. Music is inherently a human act, but today, artificial intelligence and algorithms have gotten more and more involved in music production, distribution, and marketing.

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Communication"
programme.desc: From the first written word to the Internet, we’ve continued to innovate how we communicate with each other. What happens when technology advances to the point that ideas and thoughts can be shared instantly?

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Transportation"
programme.desc: From empires to major metropolitan cities, transportation has been crucial. As cities grow more congested and overwhelmed with toxic air, how will future transportation help get us where we need to be?

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment S1 - "CUBICLE"
programme.desc: A futuristic designer, Robert Propst, dreamed up “The Action Office” as a workplace utopia that would unleash the modern knowledge worker’s vast untapped potential.

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment S1 - "BARCODE"
programme.desc: Barcodes have radically changed global commerce, helping fuel the rise of everything from massive companies like Walmart to world powers like China. And it all started with a man daydreaming on a beach in 1949.
